prompt: Write a short poem about the ocean at night.
Write a 3-sentence story about a dog who learns to fly.
Write a haiku about autumn leaves.
Describe a busy marketplace in the 1800s in one paragraph.
Write a short birthday message for a colleague.
Write a two-sentence horror story.
Write a fun limerick about a programmer.
Describe the feeling of rain using only the five senses.
Write a short motivational quote and explain it in 2 sentences.
Write a one-paragraph children's story about a curious rabbit.
Write a product description for a magical pen that writes in any language.
Write a short diary entry from the perspective of a lighthouse keeper.
Write a one-paragraph description of a futuristic city.
Write a short apology letter from a cat to its owner.
Write a fun 'about me' bio for a fictional chef who only cooks breakfast.
Write a short news headline and opening paragraph about the discovery of a new planet.
Write a recipe for 'Happiness Soup' as if it were a real dish.
Write three alternative titles for the movie Titanic.
Write a short conversation between the sun and the moon.
Write a one-paragraph fairy tale that ends with a surprising twist.
Write a 3-paragraph short story about a scientist who discovers time travel but can only go backwards by exactly one hour.
Write a villain's monologue where they genuinely believe they are the hero of the story.
Write a letter from a 90-year-old version of yourself to your current self.
Write a short story that starts with: 'The last bookstore on Earth was closing its doors.'
Write a job interview scene where the candidate is applying to be a professional dragon tamer.
Describe the same thunderstorm from two perspectives: a child seeing it for the first time and an elderly farmer.
Write a 4-stanza poem about AI using only natural metaphors — no technology words allowed.
Write a short story where the twist is revealed in the first sentence.
Write a travel guide entry for a fictional underwater city.
Write a conversation between a pessimist and an optimist stuck in an elevator.
Write a myth that explains why the sky is blue in the style of ancient Greek storytelling.
Write a short horror story set entirely inside a children's birthday party.
Write a speech a robot would give at its own retirement ceremony.
Write a short story told entirely through text messages.
Write a scene where two strangers realise they have been living parallel lives.
Write a 5-paragraph story from the perspective of an inanimate object witnessing a life-changing human moment. Do not reveal what the object is until the final sentence.
Write the same event — a couple's first argument — three times: once as tragedy, once as comedy, once as a thriller.
Write a philosophical short story exploring free will vs determinism without using those words or any direct philosophical terminology.
Write a 4-paragraph opening chapter of a novel set in a world where music has been outlawed. Establish character, setting, conflict, and tone.
Write a short story where the narrator is unreliable and the reader must piece together what actually happened from contradictions in the text.
Write a scene in which a character says goodbye without using the word goodbye or any direct synonym.
Write a story in second person that makes the reader feel implicated in a morally ambiguous decision.
Write a poem that works both as a love poem and as a description of a city, with every line serving both meanings.
Write a story where the opening and closing paragraphs use identical words but mean completely different things due to context.
Write a 3-paragraph story with no adjectives or adverbs — convey all emotion through action and dialogue only.
